body:
## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
Cross-device sync alone is table stakes, but this ticket explicitly pushes past parity by combining privacy-first encryption with conflict-safe restore. That gives us a trust-and-reliability position versus competitors that emphasize convenience sync but do not foreground user-held encryption keys and conflict-preserving recovery choices.

## Competitor evidence
- LivingWriter markets automatic cloud sync and backups as core reliability value: https://livingwriter.com/about
- Campfire positions cloud sync and backup behavior (including autosave cadence and common sync caveats), indicating this baseline already exists in-market: https://campfiretechnology.com/faq/ and https://www.campfirewriting.com/plus

## Risks / trade-offs
- Significant engineering surface area (auth, encryption key UX, conflict UI, recovery testing).
- Key management friction can hurt activation if setup is not fast and clear.
- If restore/conflict UX is weak, perceived trust can drop below simpler competitor flows.

## Recommendation
Proceed, but keep the differentiation bar explicit in Design: make trust legible (status clarity, conflict explanations, safe restore history) and keep opt-in/key setup under 2 minutes.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design
